---
title: "401_k_double_ml"
author: "Alexander Unger"
date: "2026-05-22"
output: html_document
---
```{r}

library(OutcomeWeights)  # dml_with_smoother(), get_outcome_weights()
library(cobalt)          # love.plot()
library(viridis)         # colour palette for Love plots
library(gridExtra)       # grid.arrange()
library(ggplot2)         # required by OutcomeWeights


cat(sprintf("BLOCK A ready. N=%d | mean(D)=%.3f | mean(Z)=%.3f\n\n",
            nrow(pension), mean(D), mean(Z)))


# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK A: X matrices for DML
# ==============================================================================
# DML requires a CLEAN numeric matrix without factor attributes.
# grf::validate_X() rejects matrices with lingering factor-level attributes.
# make_X() strips all attributes while preserving column names.
# ncol >= 2 required — single-column matrices fail grf in R 4.5.x.
#
# Card specification for DML (same variables as X_card_kappa, minus intercept):
#   exper, expersq, black, south, smsa, smsa66, reg661–reg668
#   This is a 14-column matrix.
#
# Kitagawa specification for DML (same variables as X_kit_kappa, minus intercept):
#   black, south, smsa, smsa66, south66
#   This is a 5-column matrix.
#
# IMPORTANT: DML X matrices have NO intercept (grf adds it internally).
#            Kappa X matrices (X_card_kappa, X_kit_kappa) retain the intercept
#            column because logit_mle() and cbps() need it.
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK A — DML SETUP FOR 401(k)
# ==============================================================================
# Design:
#   Instrument Z  = e401  (eligibility)
#   Treatment  D  = p401  (participation)
#   Outcome    Y  = net_tfa (net total financial assets)
#   Covariates X  = age, db, educ, fsize, hown, inc, male, marr, pira, twoearn
#
# One covariate spec (unlike Card which had Card + Kitagawa).
# No intercept in DML matrices — grf and DoubleML handle intercepts internally.
# ==============================================================================

make_X <- function(formula, data) {
  # Strips all attributes from model.matrix output to produce a plain numeric
  # matrix safe for grf::validate_X().
  mm <- model.matrix(formula, data = data)
  m  <- matrix(as.numeric(mm), nrow = nrow(mm), ncol = ncol(mm))
  colnames(m) <- colnames(mm)
  m
}

# 401k DML matrix (10 covariates, no intercept)
X_dml_401 <- make_X(
  ~ 0 + age + db + educ + fsize + hown + inc + male + marr + pira + twoearn,
  data = pension
)

cat("DML X matrix:\n")
cat(sprintf("  X_dml_401: %dx%d | numeric=%s\n",
            nrow(X_dml_401), ncol(X_dml_401), is.numeric(X_dml_401)))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions for DML extraction
# (identical in purpose to Card DML Block A)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

get_estimate <- function(res, row_name) {
  # Extract point estimate from OutcomeWeights summary() output.
  if ("Estimate" %in% colnames(res)) return(as.numeric(res[row_name, "Estimate"]))
  as.numeric(res[row_name, 1])
}

check_omega_rows <- function(omega_obj, rows = c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW")) {
  # Verify that the expected outcome-weight rows exist in the omega object.
  missing_rows <- setdiff(rows, rownames(omega_obj$omega))
  if (length(missing_rows) > 0)
    stop("Missing expected outcome-weight rows: ", paste(missing_rows, collapse = ", "))
}

cat("\nBLOCK A complete. Ready for BLOCKS B and C.\n\n")

```
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK B — RUN DOUBLE ML (2-fold and 5-fold cross-fitting)
# ==============================================================================
# dml_with_smoother() from OutcomeWeights runs PLR, PLR-IV, AIPW, Wald-AIPW
# simultaneously and stores the NxN smoother matrices needed for outcome weights.
# Memory note: each dml object stores several NxN matrices.
# With N=9915 this is manageable but remove objects after extracting weights.
# ==============================================================================

set.seed(1234)

cat("Running DML — 2-fold cross-fitting...\n")
dml_2f_401 <- dml_with_smoother(Y, D, X_dml_401, Z, n_cf_folds = 2)
results_dml_2f_401 <- summary(dml_2f_401)
cat("  2-fold results:\n")
print(results_dml_2f_401)

cat("\nExtracting outcome weights (2-fold)...\n")
omega_dml_2f_401 <- get_outcome_weights(dml_2f_401)
check_omega_rows(omega_dml_2f_401, rows = c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"))

# Algebraic identity check: omega'Y should reproduce point estimates exactly
cat("omega'Y replicates point estimates (2-fold)?",
    all.equal(
      as.numeric(omega_dml_2f_401$omega %*% Y),
      as.numeric(results_dml_2f_401[, 1])
    ), "\n")

# Free memory before running 5-fold
rm(dml_2f_401); gc()

cat("\nRunning DML — 5-fold cross-fitting...\n")
dml_5f_401 <- dml_with_smoother(Y, D, X_dml_401, Z, n_cf_folds = 5)
results_dml_5f_401 <- summary(dml_5f_401)
cat("  5-fold results:\n")
print(results_dml_5f_401)

cat("\nExtracting outcome weights (5-fold)...\n")
omega_dml_5f_401 <- get_outcome_weights(dml_5f_401)
check_omega_rows(omega_dml_5f_401, rows = c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"))

cat("omega'Y replicates point estimates (5-fold)?",
    all.equal(
      as.numeric(omega_dml_5f_401$omega %*% Y),
      as.numeric(results_dml_5f_401[, 1])
    ), "\n")

rm(dml_5f_401); gc()

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract named weight vectors for PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW only.
# These are reused in Blocks C, D, and E — avoids repeated indexing.
# Row names come from dml_with_smoother(): "PLR-IV" and "Wald-AIPW".
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
w_plriv_2f   <- omega_dml_2f_401$omega["PLR-IV",    ]
w_waipw_2f   <- omega_dml_2f_401$omega["Wald-AIPW", ]
w_plriv_5f   <- omega_dml_5f_401$omega["PLR-IV",    ]
w_waipw_5f   <- omega_dml_5f_401$omega["Wald-AIPW", ]

cat("\nBLOCK B complete.\n\n")

```



```{r}

# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK C — LOVE PLOTS: DML (PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW only, 2-fold vs 5-fold)
# ==============================================================================
# REMOVED: PLR and AIPW panels — not needed for thesis comparison.
# KEPT:    PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW, overlaying 2-fold vs 5-fold cross-fitting.
# Uses named weight vectors extracted at end of Block B.
# ==============================================================================

threshold <- 0.1

create_love_plot_401 <- function(title, w_2f, w_5f) {
  love.plot(
    D ~ X_dml_401,
    weights = list(
      "2-fold" = w_2f * (2 * D - 1),
      "5-fold" = w_5f * (2 * D - 1)
    ),
    position   = "bottom",
    title      = title,
    thresholds = c(m = threshold),
    var.order  = "unadjusted",
    binary     = "std",
    abs        = TRUE,
    line       = TRUE,
    colors     = viridis(3),
    shapes     = c("circle", "triangle", "diamond")
  )
}

love_plot_plriv_401  <- create_love_plot_401("PLR-IV",    w_plriv_2f, w_plriv_5f)
love_plot_waipw_401  <- create_love_plot_401("Wald-AIPW", w_waipw_2f, w_waipw_5f)

love_plot_plriv_401
love_plot_waipw_401

grid.arrange(
  love_plot_plriv_401, love_plot_waipw_401,
  nrow = 1,
  top  = "Love plots: PLR-IV vs Wald-AIPW | 401(k) | 2-fold vs 5-fold"
)

cat("\nBLOCK C complete.\n\n")





```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK D — KAPPA OUTCOME WEIGHTS: SETUP
# ==============================================================================
# Computes propensity scores and outcome weights for all kappa estimators.
# Reuses X_401_kappa from the replicate session (already in memory).
# If running standalone, X_401_kappa must be defined first (see replicate Rmd).
#
# Produces:
#   kw_401  — named list with weight vectors for all 6 kappa estimators
#   p_ml_401, p_cb_401 — propensity scores (MLE and CBPS)
# ==============================================================================

cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("BLOCK D — KAPPA OUTCOME WEIGHTS SETUP\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

# Recompute propensity scores here in case this Rmd is run standalone.
# In a shared session these are already available from the replicate Rmd,
# but recomputing is fast and makes this block self-contained.
p_ml_401 <- logit_mle(Z, X_401_kappa)
p_cb_401 <- get_cbps_p(Z, X_401_kappa)

kw_ml_401 <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_ml_401)
kw_cb_401 <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_cb_401)

# Named list — mirrors kappa_weights_bundle() from Card double_ml
kw_401 <- list(
  tau_cb_u   = kw_cb_401$w_u,
  tau_ml_u   = kw_ml_401$w_u,
  tau_ml_a10 = kw_ml_401$w_a10,
  tau_ml_a   = kw_ml_401$w_a,
  tau_ml_a1  = kw_ml_401$w_a1,
  tau_ml_a0  = kw_ml_401$w_a0
)

# Algebraic identity check: sum(w * Y) == tau_hat for all kappa estimators
cat("Algebraic identity check (sum(w * Y) == tau_hat):\n")
checks <- c(
  tau_cb_u   = isTRUE(all.equal(sum(kw_401$tau_cb_u   * Y), tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_cb_401))),
  tau_ml_u   = isTRUE(all.equal(sum(kw_401$tau_ml_u   * Y), tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_ml_401))),
  tau_ml_a10 = isTRUE(all.equal(sum(kw_401$tau_ml_a10 * Y), tau_a10(Y, Z, D, p_ml_401))),
  tau_ml_a   = isTRUE(all.equal(sum(kw_401$tau_ml_a   * Y), tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml_401, "a"))),
  tau_ml_a1  = isTRUE(all.equal(sum(kw_401$tau_ml_a1  * Y), tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml_401, "a1"))),
  tau_ml_a0  = isTRUE(all.equal(sum(kw_401$tau_ml_a0  * Y), tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml_401, "a0")))
)
for (nm in names(checks)) {
  cat(sprintf("  %-14s  %s\n", nm, if (checks[nm]) "TRUE" else "FALSE"))
}

cat("\nBLOCK D complete.\n\n")


```







```{r}

# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK E — WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS: DML (PLR-IV + Wald-AIPW) VS KAPPA
# ==============================================================================
# Mirrors the Card double_ml Block E structure.
# Combines kappa weight diagnostics (from Block D) with DML diagnostics
# (PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW only, 5-fold — the main spec for the thesis).
# Produces a single unified table for easy comparison.
# ==============================================================================

cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("BLOCK E — WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS: DML VS KAPPA\n")
cat("Sum_w = 0 => translation invariant | ESS = 1 / sum(w_i^2)\n\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

diag_rows <- rbind(
  # --- Kappa estimators ---
  weight_diag(kw_401$tau_cb_u,   "tau_cb_u      (kappa, CBPS)"),
  weight_diag(kw_401$tau_ml_u,   "tau_ml_u      (kappa, MLE)"),
  weight_diag(kw_401$tau_ml_a10, "tau_ml_a10    (kappa, MLE)"),
  weight_diag(kw_401$tau_ml_a,   "tau_ml_a      (kappa, unnorm.)"),
  weight_diag(kw_401$tau_ml_a1,  "tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, unnorm.)"),
  weight_diag(kw_401$tau_ml_a0,  "tau_ml_a0     (kappa, unnorm.)"),
  # --- DML estimators: 5-fold (main spec) ---
  weight_diag(w_plriv_5f,        "PLR-IV        (grf, 5-fold)"),
  weight_diag(w_waipw_5f,        "Wald-AIPW     (grf, 5-fold)"),
  # --- DML estimators: 2-fold (robustness) ---
  weight_diag(w_plriv_2f,        "PLR-IV        (grf, 2-fold)"),
  weight_diag(w_waipw_2f,        "Wald-AIPW     (grf, 2-fold)")
)

print(diag_rows, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nBLOCK E complete.\n\n")


```




```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK F — LOVE PLOTS: DML (PLR-IV + Wald-AIPW) VS KAPPA
# ==============================================================================
# Mirrors the Card double_ml Block F structure.
# One love.plot() helper using X_dml_401 as covariate reference.
# 8-panel grid: PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW (5-fold), + all 6 kappa estimators.
# ==============================================================================

cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
cat("BLOCK F — LOVE PLOTS: DML VS KAPPA\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")

make_love_401 <- function(title_str, w_vec) {
  # Converts signed outcome weights to the treated-vs-control convention
  # expected by cobalt::love.plot() by multiplying by (2*D - 1).
  cobalt::love.plot(
    D ~ X_dml_401,
    weights    = w_vec * (2 * D - 1),
    position   = "bottom",
    title      = title_str,
    thresholds = c(m = 0.1),
    var.order  = "unadjusted",
    binary     = "std",
    abs        = TRUE,
    line       = TRUE,
    colors     = viridis::viridis(2),
    shapes     = c("circle", "triangle")
  )
}

lp_f_1 <- make_love_401("PLR-IV (5-fold)",    w_plriv_5f)
lp_f_2 <- make_love_401("Wald-AIPW (5-fold)", w_waipw_5f)
lp_f_3 <- make_love_401("tau_cb_u",           kw_401$tau_cb_u)
lp_f_4 <- make_love_401("tau_ml_u",           kw_401$tau_ml_u)
lp_f_5 <- make_love_401("tau_ml_a10",         kw_401$tau_ml_a10)
lp_f_6 <- make_love_401("tau_ml_a",           kw_401$tau_ml_a)
lp_f_7 <- make_love_401("tau_ml_a1/t",        kw_401$tau_ml_a1)
lp_f_8 <- make_love_401("tau_ml_a0",          kw_401$tau_ml_a0)

grid.arrange(
  lp_f_1, lp_f_2, lp_f_3, lp_f_4,
  lp_f_5, lp_f_6, lp_f_7, lp_f_8,
  nrow = 2,
  top  = "Love plots: DML vs Kappa | 401(k) pension covariates"
)

cat("\nBLOCK F complete.\n\n")

```
